bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening because there is no time pressure yet and the opponent’s first offer is the minimum-utility valid bid for me on every issue. Their message is cooperative but not supported by concessions yet, so I will anchor with my best valid package while leaving room to trade later.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated the same all-valueE offer with no numeric concession. Their messages are cooperative emotional appeals, but the offer history shows they are holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while protecting the highest-weight issues.

It is still very early (t=0.04), so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent has moved only slightly from all valueE to issueA:valueD while keeping the rest at valueE, so their offers remain very low utility to me; their messages are cooperative but not enough to justify major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is little reason to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly, from all valueE to issueA:valueD while keeping the rest at valueE, and their messages are cooperative but not binding; I will concede only on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making limited concessions on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have only improved slightly for me, from all valueE to one valueD on issueA or issueB, so I read them as conceding minimally despite cooperative messages. I will hold firm on high-weight issueA, issueB, and issueC while conceding further on issueD and issueE.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility, since it is still early at t=0.10 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, but their actual offers remain very low for me with only small movement on issueA/issueB, so I am conceding mainly on low-weight issueD/issueE while holding firm on high-weight issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high utility offer around 0.88 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to concede on the high-weight issues A, B, and C yet. The opponent has only made small concessions and their latest valid offer is still very low for me, while their emotional messages do not add binding information beyond the bid history. I am therefore conceding further only on the lowest-weight issueE while holding firm on my most important issues.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86 because it is still early at t=0.14, so I should not concede on the high-weight issues A, B, and C yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and remain low-utility, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding constraints; their bidding suggests they may value concessions on the lower-weight issues D and E, so I concede there first.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA, but they remain very low overall and their emotional messages do not add binding information. I am conceding further only on low-weight issues while holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a utility around 0.80: still strong for me this early, but with a real concession beyond my prior offer by moving issueC from valueA to valueB while keeping the high-weight issues A and B at valueA. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me but remain quite low overall, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding evidence, so I am conceding gradually rather than matching their low bids.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.76 because it is early in the negotiation, so I should not concede heavily on my most important issues yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me on issueA and issueB, but they remain mostly unfavorable overall and their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding constraints. I will keep issueA and issueB at my preferred values while conceding further on the lower/mid-weight issues they appear to hold firm on.

I am targeting about 0.72 utility for myself, conceding further from my prior 0.76 offer because we are still early but moving into a more constructive phase. The opponent’s actual offers remain low for me and mostly hold issueC/issueD/issueE near valueE, so I am conceding on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB.

At t=0.24 it is still early-mid negotiation, so I am targeting a utility around 0.76 rather than making major concessions on the high-weight issues A and B. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me over time, especially with movement on issueA/issueB/issueC, but their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the bid pattern rather than the claims. I will keep issueA and issueB at my best values while conceding substantially on the lower-weight issues D and E and moderately on issueC.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.72 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding further on issueC and leaving low-weight issues D and E at values the opponent has consistently requested. At t=0.26 there is still time, but the opponent’s bids show gradual concessions on issueA and issueC while staying firm on B/D/E, so this is a measured concession without overreacting to their emotional messages.

At t=0.28 we are still early enough to target a solid utility around 0.70 rather than chase the opponent’s low-utility latest offer. The opponent’s bids show only limited concession to us, with occasional movement on issueA/issueB/issueC, so I am offering a modest concession on high-weight issueB while keeping issueA strong and leaving low-weight issueD/issueE at values they seem to prefer.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.66 while making a new concession on issueC, since the opponent has repeatedly signaled preference for lower issueC values and I can trade there before weakening issueA/issueB too much. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not jump down to their low-utility offers; their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual bids show only limited concession and a best offer to me still well below this bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.64, conceding further from my earlier high-utility positions but still preserving value on the most important issues. At t=0.32 there is time to negotiate, and the opponent’s valid offers show some movement on issueA and issueB, so I am holding issueA at valueA while moving issueB/issueC toward a middle range and leaving low-weight issueD/issueE conceded.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.58: we are only about one-third through, so I will not concede on issueA, but I can move on issueB while keeping the opponent’s preferred low-weight issueD/issueE positions. The opponent’s offers have gradually improved for me, especially on issueA and issueC, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding constraints, so I am responding to the bidding pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting about 0.66 utility: above the opponent’s latest 0.60 offer but still a meaningful concession from my earlier positions. At t=0.36 there is no need to rush to their exact bid, but their recent movement on issueA and issueB suggests a deal around those terms may be possible; I will ask for a modest improvement on issueC, which they have previously offered at valueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.62 rather than conceding to the opponent’s latest very low-utility offer, since time is still only 0.38 and there is room to negotiate. The opponent’s actual bids show they have previously been willing to offer issueA: valueB and issueB: valueA together, while usually holding issueC/issueD/issueE near valueE, so I am conceding mainly on the lower-weight and repeatedly contested issues while preserving strong value on issueB.
